shape_semantics: I2S-ANTI. code over (cmp,vp,vpc,naive) = L,W,_,W: cmplog BLOCKS, while value_profile AND naive RESOLVE (vpc non-decisive). The technique that HURTS is I2S (cmplog's input-to-state substitution): the branch is reached by plain havoc byte/token exploration (naive) and by the gradient (value_profile), but cmplog's extra I2S stage diverts or pollutes the corpus and BLOCKS it. The shape (Layer 1) already asserts I2S-anti (cmplog is the sole loser). The evidence test does NOT re-confirm that cmplog blocks (that would verify the loser-pattern with the loser-pattern, violating G2); it independently discriminates WHY I2S hurts on each branch -- whether the I2S stage (a) actively splices a WRONG/competing operand that DEPLETES the cmplog corpus of the target byte region (a measurable corpus signature on cmplog vs naive, INDEPENDENT of whether cmplog blocks), or (b) has NO harvestable input-resident operand at all (the gate value is table/state/property-derived) so I2S is pure overhead, or (c) the gate is a length/sequence-accumulation gate that cmplog's CMP-biased budget SHORTENS the input away from, or (d) the gate fires on iteration/traversal DEPTH (a loop-exit guard or a deep Ragel state-machine action) so the winner corpus drives the branch line DEEPER per seed, or (e) the comparison operand is a DECODED codepoint that is nonetheless reachable in raw input byte-space (winner lands the byte-pair, loser stalls).

hypotheses.label: I2S splices a WRONG/competing but abundant CMP operand into the input (a well-known font-table tag, a script-tag/codepoint constant, an in-range fixed-point magnitude, or a competing protocol value), pinning the cmplog corpus onto that decoy and DEPLETING it of the target byte value at the winner gate's offset; naive/value_profile never harvest the decoy so they freely reach the target

params.comment: For the branch's W/L gate offset (the divergent byte from the analysis byte-diff / per-role overlay), compute the cmplog-vs-naive log2 enrichment of the TARGET byte value (the winner-resolving value at that offset) vs the DECOY value (the competing operand I2S substitutes). Anti-direction: cmplog DEPLETES the target (signed_target_enrich < 0) and ENRICHES the decoy (decoy_enrich > 0).
params.target_value_source: signature.operand_literal (e.g. DFontTag 00 00 01 00 for 5391; PNG_FIXED_ERROR overflow byte for libpng/3977; 0x3a for openthread/13349,13391; table-tag constants for 5815/5843/5857) at the cmp-site offset from the per-role overlay

hypotheses.prediction: On the gate offset the cmplog corpus is DEPLETED of the target value relative to naive (signed_target_enrich < -0.3) AND/OR ENRICHED in the decoy operand (decoy_enrich > 0): I2S is actively steering bytes AWAY from the winner value. A pure-overhead mechanism (I2S has no operand to substitute) would predict signed_target_enrich ~ 0 and operand_enrichment returning no_gate_signature -- no directional depletion, because there is no decoy to splice. A pro-I2S mechanism would predict signed_target_enrich > 0. Only the active-decoy-substitution mechanism predicts a NEGATIVE target enrichment / positive decoy enrichment, which is what distinguishes 'I2S splices the wrong abundant operand' from 'I2S simply has nothing to do here'.

hypotheses.label: The winner gate needs havoc to BUILD or PRESERVE a long / periodically-repeated glyph-record byte sequence (multi-matra record runs, long multi-glyph Thai state-machine traversal); the gate value is table/state-derived (no harvestable operand), but cmplog's CMP-biased budget produces SHORTER, heterogeneous inputs that never reach the required sequence length, while naive's byte-expansion/copy operators grow and sustain it

params.comment: Reuse joint_necessity's struct-size half: take the median seed SIZE of the winner-resolving (naive/value_profile) seed set vs the cmplog-BLOCKING seed set. size_lift = median(resolving_size) / median(blocking_size). The length/accumulation subtype predicts the resolving seeds are materially LONGER than the blocking seeds. --tokens is unused for this hypothesis (size only); the token-count half of the tool is ignored here.

tokens_by_target.harfbuzz: (size-only; token vocabulary not used for this hypothesis)

hypotheses.prediction: The winner-resolving seeds are materially LONGER than the cmplog-blocking seeds (size_lift >= 1.3): the divergence is driven by sequence/length accumulation that cmplog's CMP-biased, shorter inputs fail to build. A no-operand-overhead mechanism (subtype 3 below) would predict size_lift ~ 1.0 -- cmplog and naive reach SIMILARLY-sized inputs and the gate is simply a derived category cmplog can't aim at, with no length asymmetry. A decoy-substitution mechanism would have a fixed-offset operand signature (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature). This length asymmetry, on a gate with NO harvestable operand, is what separates 'I2S shortens the input below the accumulation threshold' from plain 'I2S overhead on a derived gate'.

hypotheses.label: The gate value is computed from shaping tables, Unicode-property lookups, Ragel state-machine internals, or derived category classification -- NOT a literal value compared against input bytes anywhere. I2S has no input-resident CMP operand to harvest or substitute; its per-execution callback overhead and misdirected substitution budget crowd out the broad havoc byte exploration that resolves the branch, with NO directional corpus depletion and NO length asymmetry

params.comment: operand_enrichment is asked for a gate signature; on these branches it returns no_gate_signature because the gate is NOT a fixed-offset value comparison (it is a derived/table/state category). The DIAGNOSTIC for this hypothesis is the ABSENCE of a harvestable operand (skip == no_gate_signature) COMBINED WITH the absence of a length asymmetry (the i2s_disrupts_havoc_sequence_accumulation hypothesis, tried first, did NOT fire). This is the residual / catch-all I2S-anti subtype.

hypotheses.prediction: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (no fixed-offset operand to harvest) AND there is no resolving-vs-blocking length asymmetry (size_lift < 1.3, so the sequence-accumulation hypothesis is ruled out): I2S simply has nothing to substitute and is pure overhead. A decoy-substitution mechanism would instead yield a fixed-offset operand with negative target enrichment (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature, signed_target_enrich < 0). A sequence-accumulation mechanism would yield size_lift >= 1.3. The conjunction no_gate_signature AND size_lift<1.3 is exactly what isolates 'derived gate, no operand, no length effect -- just I2S overhead' from the two operand/length subtypes.

hypotheses.label: The winner gate is a loop-exit guard or a deep Ragel state-machine action whose firing depends on how DEEP the input drives the per-buffer traversal (delete_glyphs_inplace backward-cluster loop reaching its false exit; find_syllables_myanmar walking the state machine into a late case action). There is no input-resident CMP operand (operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature) and no whole-input length asymmetry (size_lift < 1.3, so the sequence-accumulation hypothesis does NOT fire); the distinguishing signal is per-seed iteration DEPTH at the branch line: the winner (naive/value_profile) corpus drives the branch line DEEPER per seed, while cmplog's CMP-biased budget reaches it far fewer times. I2S overhead here is paid as fewer/shallower traversals, not as a competing operand or a shorter input.

params.comment: Run the winner-resolving vs loser-blocking seed sets through the libafl-harfbuzz-cov image and read the per-seed-normalized execution count of the branch line (the loop guard at delete_glyphs_inplace:633 for 5311; the case-action line in find_syllables_myanmar:502 for 5931). depth_lift = winner_depth / loser_depth. The iteration-depth subtype predicts the winner drives the branch line materially deeper per seed.

hypotheses.prediction: The winner corpus drives the branch line DEEPER per seed (winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.3): the divergence is iteration/traversal depth that cmplog's CMP-substitution budget under-drives, NOT a competing operand (operand_enrichment returned no_gate_signature -- there is no fixed-offset value to deplete) and NOT a whole-input length effect (size_lift < 1.3 -- the input is not materially longer, it is traversed more deeply per byte). A pure no-operand-overhead gate (subtype 3) would predict depth_lift ~ 1.0 -- cmplog reaches the branch line about as deeply per seed and simply cannot aim at the derived category, with no per-seed depth asymmetry. A sequence-accumulation gate would have shown its asymmetry as raw size (size_lift >= 1.3) rather than per-seed depth at a fixed-length input. This per-seed depth advantage, on a gate with no operand and no length lift, is what separates 'cmplog under-drives the traversal' from 'cmplog just has nothing to substitute'.
hypotheses.rule: skip == 'no_gate_signature' AND size_lift < 1.3 AND winner_deeper == true AND depth_lift >= 1.3

hypotheses.label: The gate is an equality on a value DECODED inside the shaper (a UTF/codepoint slot compared to a small set of constants, e.g. Thai consonants 0x0E1B/0x0E1D/0x0E1F), so I2S harvests the comparison operand but rewrites the WRONG input offsets (the decoded codepoint does not sit at the contiguous input field I2S logs); yet the target value IS reachable in raw input byte-space as a short byte-pair the winner (naive/value_profile) lands by undirected mutation while the loser stalls. Distinct from decoy-substitution because operand_enrichment finds NO directional fixed-offset depletion (the byte does not live at a stable corpus offset), and distinct from pure derived-gate overhead because the operand value IS approachable in byte-space.

params.comment: operand value = the decoded codepoint byte-pair(s) from signature.operand_literal: 0x0E1B/0x0E1D/0x0E1F as little-endian byte-pairs (1b 0e / 1d 0e / 1f 0e) for harfbuzz/5954 (width 2). Slide a 2-byte window over each arm's seeds; per-arm min Hamming distance to the nearest matching byte-pair. Winner (naive/vp) lands the pair; loser (cmplog) stalls at a non-matching low byte next to 0x0E.
params.width_caveat: operand width 2 is chance-alignment prone (registry caveat); require a non-trivial distance_gap (>= 0.15) so a coincidental near-match does not pass.

hypotheses.prediction: The winner arm lands materially CLOSER to the operand byte-pair than the loser (winner_closer == true AND distance_gap >= 0.15): the decoded codepoint IS reachable in raw byte-space and the winner's undirected mutation lands it while cmplog's I2S-substituted wrong offsets stall. A decoy-substitution mechanism would instead show a fixed-offset corpus depletion (operand_enrichment != no_gate_signature with signed_target_enrich < -0.3) -- here that test does NOT fire because the byte does not live at a stable offset. A pure derived-gate-overhead mechanism (no byte-reachable operand at all) would predict winner_closer == false / distance_gap ~ 0 -- no arm gets meaningfully closer in byte-space. The winner's byte-space proximity advantage to a DECODED operand is exactly what isolates this from both neighbours.

hypotheses.label: (decidable:false) The gate is a post-shaping / decoded-property predicate with NO input-resident operand and NO byte-reachable literal: a multi-codepoint conjunct (ZWJ followed by Extended_Pictographic), a post-shaping script enum inequality (script != MALAYALAM && != TAMIL), or a fixed-prefix glyph-category trigraph (Ra/As/H at slots start..start+2). The hypothesised effect is I2S OVER-SOLVING the comparison (it harvests the decoded operand and over-commits the corpus to the true side, starving the winner's false-side reach), but no BUILT measurement can confirm this: operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature (no fixed input offset), value_distance_reached has no usable byte-reachable literal (script enums / decoded conjuncts are not raw input fields), there is no length asymmetry (not a sequence-accumulation gate), and these fire at a fixed buffer prefix rather than at traversal depth (depth_reach shows no per-seed depth advantage). It is therefore indistinguishable from generic I2S overhead with current tools.

params.comment: These branches were the over-solve hint members in the prior round (5723/5881) plus the fixed-prefix Myanmar trigraph (5934). Their gates compare DECODED/post-shaping values (UTF-decoded codepoints, table-derived script enum, table-derived glyph categories) that have no stable input offset and no byte-reachable literal usable by value_distance_reached. operand_enrichment returns no_gate_signature; there is no length lift (excludes sequence-accumulation) and no per-seed depth lift (excludes the iteration-depth subtype -- these fire at a fixed buffer prefix, not deep in a traversal). No built tool can separate the over-solve story from generic I2S overhead, so they are honestly left non-discriminable.

hypotheses.prediction: No built measurement discriminates the over-solve mechanism from generic I2S overhead here: operand_enrichment == no_gate_signature (no fixed-offset operand), value_distance_reached has no byte-reachable literal, size_lift < 1.3 (no length asymmetry), and depth_lift ~ 1.0 (no per-seed depth advantage -- the predicate fires at a fixed buffer prefix). Honest decidable:false: do NOT force a label.

data_realities_respected: No seed_lineage 'I2S event' is used anywhere -- I2S is untagged in mutation_op. The decoy signature is read from cmplog CORPUS byte frequencies (operand_enrichment), the depth signal from per-seed branch-line execution counts (depth_reach), the value-distance signal from seed byte windows (value_distance_reached) -- never from a lineage gradient/I2S event.
value_profile is treated as a feedback, not a mutator: its effect is read only via what the resolving corpus REACHES (size, byte-distance, branch-line depth), never via a lineage event.

byte_freq_ratio (operand_enrichment) is used ONLY for the fixed-offset VALUE-gate subtype (decoy-substitution), where W and L seeds align at the gate offset. The sequence-accumulation subtype uses struct_size_ratio (different-SIZED seeds), the iteration-depth subtype uses per-seed depth_reach, and the decoded-codepoint subtype uses value_distance_reached sliding windows -- none assumes byte alignment on a structural gate.

On-disk corpus / cov-image dependence: operand_enrichment, value_distance_reached, depth_reach all need on-disk corpora or the target cov image. For this shape those exist for harfbuzz and openthread on s4; libpng/3977 (operand_enrichment) is scored only if its decisive offset is available from the 286-rep seed sets, else it falls to fallback=inconclusive. Cross-server: a branch is NOT marked decidable:false merely because its corpus is off-server -- the owning server arbitrates its own targets.

Seed floor: operand_enrichment / value_distance_reached / depth_reach need >=3 W and >=3 L seeds in db/blockers.sqlite. A no_gate_signature or null-metric return caused by <3 seeds is a DATA reason (treat as inconclusive), NOT confirmation of a no-operand or non-discriminable hypothesis.
notes:
ROUND-1 re-design (loop-until-dry). SUPERSET/FREEZE: all THREE prior KEEP hypotheses are carried forward VERBATIM (i2s_decoy_substitution_target_depletion, i2s_disrupts_havoc_sequence_accumulation, i2s_overhead_on_derived_gate_no_operand) so the 23 confirmed branches re-validate unchanged -- their rules and decision precedence are untouched at the front of decision_order. I only ADDED hypotheses for the 6 UNLABELED branches (harfbuzz 5311/5723/5881/5931/5934/5954), all of which failed all 3 prior rules.

WHY THE 6 FAILED THE PRIOR RULES: their gates compare a value DECODED/DERIVED inside the shaper (UTF-decoded codepoint, post-shaping script enum, Ragel state action, loop-iteration cluster-id, fixed-prefix glyph trigraph) rather than a contiguous input field. So operand_enrichment finds no fixed-offset depletion (rule 1 can't fire), and either there is no length asymmetry or the asymmetry is per-seed depth rather than raw size (rules 2/3 mis-evaluate or come out flat). They split into THREE genuinely different residual mechanisms:

NEW HYP A -- i2s_overhead_on_iteration_depth_gate (5311, 5931): the gate fires on traversal DEPTH (delete_glyphs_inplace backward-cluster loop-exit; find_syllables_myanmar late case action). Discriminated by depth_reach (the registry's built iteration-depth tool, anchored on harfbuzz/6585): winner_deeper AND depth_lift>=1.3, gated behind no_gate_signature AND size_lift<1.3 so it is tried AFTER the sequence-accumulation length test and only on operand-less gates. G1: a pure-overhead gate predicts depth_lift~1.0; a length gate predicts size_lift>=1.3 -- only this one predicts a per-seed depth advantage with NO size lift.

NEW HYP B -- i2s_decoded_codepoint_byte_reachable_gate (5954): equality on a decoded Thai codepoint (0x0E1B/D/F) that I2S can't aim at (wrong offsets) but that IS reachable as a raw byte-pair the winner lands. Discriminated by value_distance_reached (built vp-gradient tool): winner_closer AND distance_gap>=0.15 (the 0.15 floor honors the registry's width<=2 chance-alignment caveat). G1: a decoy gate would show fixed-offset depletion (operand_enrichment fires); a pure-derived gate would show winner_closer==false / distance_gap~0 -- only this predicts a winner byte-space proximity advantage to a decoded operand.

DECIDABLE:FALSE SUB-GROUP -- i2s_oversolve_postshaping_derived_predicate (5723, 5881, 5934): post-shaping conjunct (ZWJ+pictographic), post-shaping script-enum inequality (!=Malayalam && !=Tamil), and fixed-prefix Ra/As/H trigraph. The hypothesised effect is I2S OVER-SOLVING the decoded predicate (over-committing to the true side, starving the false-side winner), but NO built tool can confirm it: operand_enrichment->no_gate_signature (no input offset), value_distance_reached has no byte-reachable literal (script enums / decoded conjuncts are not raw input fields), no length asymmetry, and they fire at a FIXED buffer prefix so depth_reach shows no per-seed depth advantage. Per G3 this is an honest non-discriminable group (decidable:false, rule:'false') -- it would need a per-call-context / decoded-value-provenance tool that is not in the registry. I did NOT invent a mechanism to force a label.

DECISION ORDER: the 3 frozen priors first (decoy-substitution, sequence-accumulation, no-operand overhead-catch-all is placed LAST so the two new specific tests are tried before the catch-all), then iteration-depth, then decoded-codepoint-byte-reachable, then the no-operand catch-all. The decidable:false group has rule 'false' and never matches, falling to fallback=inconclusive. NOTE: i2s_overhead_on_derived_gate_no_operand (catch-all, rule no_gate_signature AND size_lift<1.3) is moved to the END of decision_order so a 5311/5931-style depth gate gets the more specific depth_reach test first; this does not change its rule, only its precedence, and the confirmed 13 catch-all members still match it (none of them is winner_deeper, so the depth hypothesis won't claim them).

G2 throughout: every new measurement reads what the winner-vs-loser CORPUS / seed-set REACHES (per-seed branch-line depth; seed byte-window distance), never the resolve pattern that defined the shape. PRIORS KEPT: 3 (all). NEW MECHANISMS: 2 discriminable (iteration-depth via depth_reach; decoded-codepoint-byte-reachable via value_distance_reached). DECIDABLE:FALSE: 1 sub-group of 3 branches (post-shaping over-solve, no built discriminator).
